text: A company is a collection of algorithms, where each business workflow is a series of steps that can be broken down recursively into sub-algorithms.

evidence.quote: you will recognize this as a series of steps, aka—an algorithm

issues.detail: 2 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: The author conceptualizes a business as a graph of algorithms—a way of thinking about the relationships between components using actions like send to or receive.

evidence.quote: I think of this as a graph of algorithms

text: The word 'graph' is used because it enables thinking about relationships between components and describing them using actions such as send to or receive.

evidence.quote: I use the word graph because it allows me to think of the relationships between the different components and describe them using an action—such as send to or receive, or whatever

text: Breaking down business processes into component algorithms reveals all the pieces, how they fit together, and what can be optimized or eliminated.

evidence.quote: It allows you to see all the pieces, how they fit together, and figure out what to optimize or even eliminate from the workflow

issues.detail: 1 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: AI excels at both discrete task execution and determining how things fit together, making every workflow component ripe for optimization or elimination.

evidence.quote: AI excels at both discrete task execution and determining how things fit together, and every single one of your company's workflow components becomes ripe for optimization or elimination

text: AI-deniers fail to realize how opaque most businesses are, how much waste exists in their processes, and the level of redundancy and inefficiency present.

evidence.quote: AI-deniers are simply not realizing how opaque most businesses are, how much waste there is in their processes, or the level of redundancy and inefficiency

text: Consultancies such as Accenture, KPMG, and McKinsey will lead the push to analyze and optimize businesses using AI.

evidence.quote: consultancies like Accenture, KPMG, and McKinsey will be leading that push

text: The result of AI-driven business optimization will inevitably be a smaller, tighter company that saves money and requires fewer humans to run it.

evidence.quote: the result will inevitably be a smaller, tighter company that not only saves money but has fewer humans required to run it

text: Once AI is used inside a company for workflow analysis, it is one step away from a system that continuously analyzes all business components and algorithms.

evidence.quote: it's just one step away from a workflow that continuously analyzes these business components/algorithms

text: Every business process—from idea generation to campaign launching and lead capturing—is just another algorithm that AI will soon execute better than most humans.

evidence.quote: AI will do so much of this very soon, often better than most humans

text: No matter how special a product or company appears, it still operates as a pipeline of steps that can be broken down algorithmically.

evidence.quote: No matter how special the product or company, it still operates as a pipeline of steps

text: The upside of AI-driven business optimization is that businesses will become far more efficient, producing more productivity, output, and new things in the world.

evidence.quote: businesses are about to become way more efficient. Which means more productivity, more output, and ultimately a lot more new things in the world

issues.detail: quote spans a window `p067.s001`..`p068.s002` around the ref

text: The downside of AI-driven business optimization will be the loss of many easy-to-automate human jobs.

evidence.quote: there will be downsides—especially from the loss of many easy-to-automate human jobs

text: Business owners and workers should realize what is coming and prepare for it both as competitors in the new model and as participants in these companies.

evidence.quote: My advice is not to be depressed, but instead to realize what's happening and prepare for it

issues.detail: 2 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: Business owners and employees should understand what their business looks like as a graph of algorithms and start anticipating what AI will recommend before it arrives.

evidence.quote: Understand what your business looks like as a Graph of Algorithms, and start thinking about what AI will recommend before it gets there

issues.detail: 2 argument(s) not found in the quote/span — advisory only

text: The process of AI-driven business optimization will make it easier for people to start businesses, including those who had no chance in 2022 or before.

evidence.quote: This same process will also make it easier for people to start businesses, including those who had no chance in 2022 or before

text: The author believes AI is fueled by transparency, meaning that making business processes visible enables AI to optimize them.

evidence.quote: AI is about to make this process commonplace for all businesses because companies can't wait to optimize using AI, and AI is fueled by transparency

text: I think the reason so many people don't understand how big AI is going to be is that they don't understand that everything is an algorithm.

evidence.quote: I think the reason so many people don't understand how big AI is going to be is that they don't understand that everything is an algorithm.

text: Explainability is the new currency.

evidence.quote: Explainability is the new currency.

text: Transparency opens the door for optimization

evidence.quote: Transparency opens the door for optimization

text: Steps that can be articulated and visualized.

evidence.quote: Steps that can be articulated and visualized.
